title: Art Theft in Amazonia
source_text:
Title: Art Theft in Amazonia
Crime Type: theft
Setting: Amazon rainforest / remote research station (contemporary)
Environment: jungle_outpost
Genres: heist, wilderness, artifact_recovery

Summary:
A priceless Pre-Columbian artifact vanishes from a remote anthropological research station deep in the Amazon. The theft happened during a tropical storm that cut off communications. The artifact may have never left the jungle — or the station.

Structural Elements Present:
  - crime_type
  - setting_element
  - motive
  - suspect_archetype
  - red_herring
  - reveal_mechanic
  - social_dynamic
  - evidence_type

crime.value: Theft of a priceless Pre-Columbian artifact from a remote anthropological research station during a tropical storm
crime.quote: A priceless Pre-Columbian artifact vanishes from a remote anthropological research station deep in the Amazon.

victim.value: The anthropological research station (institutional victim); the artifact itself represents the violated party — no individual victim is named
victim.quote: A priceless Pre-Columbian artifact vanishes from a remote anthropological research station deep in the Amazon.

closed_world.value: A remote research station deep in the Amazon rainforest, isolated by a tropical storm that cut off communications — the artifact likely never left this bounded environment
closed_world.quote: The theft happened during a tropical storm that cut off communications. The artifact may have never left the jungle — or the station.

culprit_and_motive.value: Culprit unspecified in source; motive is implied (artifact recovery/heist genre suggests financial gain, black-market sale, or ideological claim to the artifact)

resolution.value: Not specified in source text; the artifact recovery genre suggests the artifact is ultimately located within the station or jungle
resolution.quote: The artifact may have never left the jungle — or the station.

investigator.value: Not explicitly named or described in the source text

suspect_architecture.value: Suspect pool is confined to personnel present at the research station during the storm; the closed-world setting implies a small, identifiable group of researchers, staff, or visitors — consistent with 'suspect_archetype' structural element noted

red_herring.value: The tropical storm and severed communications serve as a potential red herring, suggesting an outside theft or escape route when the artifact likely never left the station
red_herring.quote: The artifact may have never left the jungle — or the station.

clue_fairness.value: Evidence type is listed as a structural element present, suggesting clues are embedded in the narrative; physical evidence within the jungle outpost environment would be accessible to the reader

social_world.value: A small, isolated community of anthropologists and station personnel in a remote jungle outpost — tension likely arises from professional rivalry, cultural ownership disputes, or insider betrayal

alibi.value: The tropical storm provides a natural cover event that complicates establishing timelines and alibis for all station personnel during the theft window
alibi.quote: The theft happened during a tropical storm that cut off communications.

reveal_mechanic.value: Listed explicitly as a structural element present; likely involves physical evidence or a logical deduction that exposes where the artifact was hidden and by whom within the station

media_and_audience.value: Not addressed in the source text; the remote jungle setting and communications blackout effectively eliminate media presence during the crime
media_and_audience.quote: The theft happened during a tropical storm that cut off communications.

investigator_wound.value: Not specified in the source text
